data.title: Microsoft Stock (MSFT) Plunges 10% — But Here's What Crowd Wisdom Shows
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) shares slid more than 10% in Thursday's session despite better-than-expected earnings, marking the stock's worst one-day drop since March 2020. The stock is stabilizing in early Friday trading, but concerns around near-term cloud growth and rising AI spending remain in focus. Still, TipRanks' Crowd Wisdom data suggests many investors are treating the pullback as a buying opportunity rather than an exit signal.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Market Digests Microsoft's Earnings

Microsoft reported adjusted earnings of $4.14 per share, beating Wall Street estimates of $3.91. Meanwhile, revenue rose 17% year over year to $81.27 billion, also topping expectations. At the same time, cloud demand remained strong, with commercial bookings up 230%, driven by large commitments from OpenAI and Anthropic, along with steady demand for Azure.

Even after the earnings beat, the stock sold off sharply. Investors appeared worried that demand for AI services is rising faster than Microsoft can supply it, limiting near-term revenue growth. Management also said capital spending will remain elevated as the company builds more data-center capacity, raising concerns about margins in the coming quarters.

What Does TipRanks' Crowd Wisdom Show?

Despite the sharp drop, broader investor behavior tells a different story. Microsoft holds a Positive investor sentiment, based on the activity of more than 851,000 investors tracked by TipRanks in the recent quarter.

TipRanks' Crowd Wisdom tool shows that 12% of all tracked portfolios currently hold Microsoft, with investors allocating an average of 9.34% of their portfolios to the stock. More notably, portfolio additions have continued, with holdings rising 0.5% over the past seven days and 2.4% over the past 30 days.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: 'Big Year for AWS,' Wedbush Reiterates Buy on Amazon Stock (AMZN) Ahead of Q4 Earnings
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) is heading into its Q4 earnings report with strong support from Wall Street. Wedbush's five-star analyst Scott Devitt has reiterated his Buy rating on the stock and kept his $340 price target, implying roughly 40% upside. Devitt said 2026 could be a "big year for AWS," which could help push the stock toward his price target as the year unfolds. He also reaffirmed Amazon as Wedbush's top e-commerce pick for 2026.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Overall, he is optimistic ahead of Amazon's earnings report, pointing to strong growth in AWS, steady performance in the core retail business, and robust advertiser demand.

Wedbush Stays Bullish on AMZN

Devitt noted that Amazon's cloud business, AWS, grew faster than expected, with a rising backlog and expanding capacity signaling continued strong demand. He also said investors are likely to focus on continued momentum in AWS and new AI opportunities, Amazon's progress toward higher profitability and margins, spending on infrastructure and AI, and ongoing growth in its advertising business.

According to Devitt,  Amazon has multiple ways to boost profits over time, including improving fulfillment efficiency and focusing more on higher-margin areas like AWS and advertising. He also pointed to potential growth drivers, including using automation and robotics in logistics, expanding AWS infrastructure, monetizing AI in Alexa and Rufus, generating revenue from Project Kuiper, and raising Prime subscription prices.

Wedbush Raises Q4 Estimates

Devitt and his team at Wedbush raised their estimates, forecasting Amazon's Q4 operating income at $25.2 billion, with an 11.8% margin—slightly above consensus. For the full year, analysts now expect operating income of $103 billion, with a 12.8% margin.

In Q3 2025, Amazon's operating income was $17.4 billion. Below is a screenshot showing Amazon's operating income across different business units.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: SanDisk Stock Explodes 23% on Q2 Earnings Beat — Buy SNDK or Wait?
data.description:
SanDisk (SNDK) stock jumped sharply after the company delivered a standout Q2 earnings report and guidance, blowing past Wall Street estimates on both profit and revenue. Having said that, the stock's solid rally raises the risk of near-term volatility as some investors lock in profits. For long-term investors who believe AI-driven storage demand is still in the early innings, any pullbacks could present attractive entry points. Overall, SNDK remains compelling for patient investors—but chasing the stock immediately after a big spike may require caution.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

SNDK stock jumped 17.17% in pre-market trading on Friday. Year-to-date, the shares are up more than 125%.

AI Boom Powers SanDisk Profit and Revenue Surge

SanDisk's fiscal second-quarter profit jumped 672% year-over-year to $803 million, or $5.15 per share. Meanwhile, revenue climbed 61% to $3.03 billion, beating analysts' $2.69 billion estimate.

The company's data-center revenue jumped 76% from a year earlier, as artificial intelligence continued to drive a sharp increase in demand.

What Lies Ahead for SanDisk?

Looking ahead, the company said it expects revenue of $4.4 billion to $4.8 billion for the next quarter, far above Wall Street's $2.9 billion estimate. Even the midpoint of the range implies nearly 60% upside versus expectations. SanDisk also guided for adjusted EPS of $12 to $14, compared with analysts' forecasts of just $5.11—a gap of more than 150%.

SanDisk's outlook underscores just how quickly AI-driven demand is accelerating, suggesting that earnings momentum is still building rather than peaking. Despite the strong quarter, management said the company was unable to fully meet surging demand and expects this supply-demand imbalance to persist into 2026. That level of visibility supports continued revenue growth and sustained pricing power.

At the same time, SanDisk's sharp improvement in profitability signals that operating leverage is kicking in as volumes rise.

What Is the Price Target for SanDisk?

Wall Street analysts currently rate SanDisk stock a Moderate Buy, with 10 Buy ratings and four Holds issued over the past three months. After the recent rally, the shares are now trading above SanDisk's average price target of $395.93, implying a downside of 26.6% from current levels.

That said, these ratings and targets are likely to change as analysts update their forecasts following the latest earnings report.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Roblox Stock (RBLX) Slides as Dutch Open Probe into Potential Violent and Sexual Imagery
data.description:
Shares in online gaming group Roblox (RBLX) were lower today as Dutch regulators opened a probe into whether it is exposing children to violent and sexual imagery.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Potential Roblox Risks

The Netherlands Authority for Consumers and Markets (ACM) said it has launched an investigation into the gaming platform and would examine "potential risks to underage users in the EU." It said the probe would likely last around 12 months.

"The platform regularly makes the news, for example, due to concerns about violent or sexually explicit games that minors are exposed to," the ACM said in a statement.

Other concerns include reports of ill-intentioned adults targeting children on the platform and the use of misleading techniques to encourage purchases.

The ACM said it had also received reports of these allegations and "considers this sufficient reason to launch a formal investigation into possible violations of the rules by Roblox."

Under the EU's Digital Services Act (DSA), platforms must take "appropriate and proportionate measures" to ensure a high level of safety and privacy for minors.

The ACM said it could impose a "binding instruction, fine, or penalty" on Roblox if it finds that it has been breaking the rules.

The ACM certainly has teeth. In 2024 it fined Fortnite maker Epic Games €1.1 million because it ruled that vulnerable children had been exploited and pressured into making purchases in the game's Item Shop.

Roblox Responds

In response to the ACM probe, Roblox said that it was strongly committed to complying with the DSA rules and was looking forward to providing further clarity on its actions.

"We have invested significantly in building robust systems that align with the DSA's principles, including working to ensure the safety, security and privacy of minors," Roblox said.

It pointed out recent measures taken on the platform, such as introducing age check requirements for all users wishing to chat with others.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/30/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Fall after Microsoft Drags Down Nasdaq and S&P
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell early Friday after a 10% share price drop in the tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) dragged down the Nasdaq and S&P 500. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 1.01%, 0.80%, and 0.76%, respectively, at 3:24 a.m. EST on January 30.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Thursday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed mixed. The S&P 500 closed down 0.13%, the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite dropped 0.72%, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average gained 0.11%.

After the market closed, Apple (AAPL) shares jumped after exceeding fiscal first-quarter expectations, backed by robust iPhone sales. The company guided for revenue to grow between 13% and 16% year-over-year in Q2FY26. Moreover, SanDisk (SNDK) stock rallied 17% after beating Q2FY26 estimates and giving solid guidance.

Looking forward, investors will watch closely as President Donald Trump announces his pick to succeed Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell on Friday morning, heightening market uncertainty.

On the earnings front, results from SoFi Technologies (SOFI), ExxonMobil (XOM), Verizon (VZ), Chevron (CVX), and American Express (AXP) will be released today.

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.27%. WTI crude oil futures were trending lower, hovering near $64.14 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price decreased to nearly $5,154 per ounce on Friday, stalling a long-running rally.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened mixed today amid a flood of corporate earnings releases.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Lower

Asia-Pacific markets traded mostly lower on January 30, tracking Wall Street losses.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 2.07%. In China, the Shanghai Composite declined 0.96%, while the Shenzhen Component lost 1.32%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei slid 0.10%, but the Topix rose 0.59%.

data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: 'We Can't Ignore It,' Says Top Analyst about Broadcom; Upgrades AVGO Stock to Buy
data.description:
Broadcom Limited (AVGO) stock got a bullish upgrade from Wolfe Research after the firm raised its rating to Buy from Neutral and set a $400 price target, implying a 21% upside from current levels. The firm's top analyst Chris Caso said the call reflects fast-growing demand for Alphabet's (GOOGL) Tensor Processing Units (TPUs).

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"We can no longer ignore the growth and strength of TPUs," Caso said, adding that checks suggest annual shipments could reach 7 million units by 2028.

Why Caso Upgraded AVGO Stock to Buy

Caso said Google's decision to offer TPUs to outside customers marks a major shift in the AI chip market. As a result, TPUs are emerging as a real alternative to Nvidia's (NVDA) GPUs, positioning Broadcom as one of the key beneficiaries of this change.

Based on this view, Wolfe raised its long-term outlook for the company. The firm now expects Broadcom to generate $154.5 billion in revenue and $16 in earnings per share by 2027, reflecting stronger demand for AI-related chips. Caso also noted that there could be further upside from additional AI programs that are not yet fully reflected in current estimates.

In line with this view, Wolfe increased its 2026 AI chip revenue forecast to about $44 billion, driven by roughly 3.3 million TPU shipments. Looking ahead, the firm now sees 2027 AI revenue rising to $78.4 billion, supported by expected shipments of around 5.1 million units.

Beyond chips, networking is another important growth driver. Wolfe expects AI-related networking revenue to rise 75% in 2026, followed by 55% growth in 2027, as data-center spending remains strong.

Valuation Still Looks Reasonable

Despite the strong rally in AI stocks, Wolfe believes Broadcom's valuation remains reasonable. Caso said the company is well positioned to benefit from continued growth in AI spending over the coming years. 

Is AVGO a Good Stock to Buy? 

Turning to Wall Street, AVGO stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 29 Buys, one Hold, and zero Sell ratings. The average AVGO price target is $457.75, implying 38.41% upside potential in 2026.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Raymond James Turns Bullish on SanDisk Stock (SNDK), Says 'Arguably One of the Most Delayed Upgrades in History'
data.description:
SanDisk (SNDK) stock rallied 20% in Friday's pre-market trading after the data storage device maker reported market-beating Q2 FY26 results and issued a solid outlook, driven by data center-led demand. Following the Q2 print, top Raymond James analyst Melissa Fairbanks upgraded SNDK stock to Buy from Hold with a price target of $725, calling the move "arguably one of the most delayed upgrades in history."

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Raymond James Analyst Says Buy SanDisk Stock

While SanDisk has rallied 127% year-to-date (as of Thursday's closing), Fairbanks expects further upside, driven by strong demand and higher pricing from an "unprecedented" data center and AI cycle. Fairbanks added that SanDisk's demand is "exceptionally strong and likely only growing," as supply tightens to the level where it could be sold out for the year.

Furthermore, the 5-star analyst expects limited new supply to come online in the near term, which should support continued strength in pricing. While Fairbanks has recently favored DRAM suppliers, she now sees a more sustainable, encouraging environment for NAND as well. Consequently, Fairbanks views SanDisk as a major beneficiary of a mix-shift toward longer-lived data center builds, with supply being prioritized away from faster-turn edge and consumer markets.

Morgan Stanley Boosts Price Target

Additionally, Morgan Stanley analyst Joseph Moore boosted the price target for SanDisk stock to $690 from $483 and reiterated a Buy rating, saying, "This is the strongest upcycle we have seen in our 30 years covering the memory industry and SanDisk results tonight are yet another reminder of that."

Moore noted that with SanDisk's Q3 FY26 top-line outlook of more than 52% sequential growth, pricing has to be up 60% or more sequentially. The 5-star analyst contended that the 15% quarter-over-quarter growth in margins, which is much less than the 60% growth in the average selling price (ASP), indicates that the $13 EPS guidance (2.5x consensus) is conservative.

IS SNDK Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Overall, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on SanDisk stock based on 12 Buys and four Holds. The average SNDK stock price target of $455.31 indicates 16% downside risk.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju
